turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: It's the school's presentation. Next week, we should have the speech in front of other people, of our classmates

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Got it. What’s the topic of your group presentation?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Maybe about healthcare in different countries and what type do you have responsibility for?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Interesting topic! So, do you mean comparing how healthcare systems work in different countries? Which country are you planning to talk about?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Maybe we can talk about Pakistan or Japan, which is better. I think Japan is a developed country and Pakistan is a traditional developing country. They have different country situation.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s a good contrast — it’ll make your presentation more interesting. Which part of the healthcare systems do you want to focus on — like cost, accessibility, or quality of care?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Maybe we can talk about both of them, and I don't know which part can show the quality of a country's healthcare, maybe life expectancy, or others, I don't know.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Exactly, life expectancy is a good indicator. You could also look at infant mortality rate, number of doctors per person, and how much the government spends on healthcare. Would you like to include some personal opinions too, or just statistics?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, we better have a survey to collect personal opinions. And who will do the slide and who will complete our presentation's writing?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Good idea. Surveys make it more engaging. How many people are in your group, and do you already know what each person is good at — like design, writing, or speaking?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I don't know, but I prefer to make the introduction or give the conclusion. I think it's better for me because I can't remember the concrete data.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That makes sense — introduction and conclusion need clear speaking, not too many numbers. Would you feel more comfortable opening the presentation or giving the final summary?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I feel comfortable if you give this task for me.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Perfect! You can take the conclusion part then — it’s a great way to end strongly. Would you like some help writing what to say for your conclusion?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I can do it by myself, and our deadline is next week Sunday, so maybe we should finish before next week Wednesday and have a meeting to exchange our opinions. If you have some problems, you can ask me on the Teams and send the message. When I see it, I will reply you quickly.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sounds like a solid plan! Meeting before Wednesday gives everyone time to adjust things. What time on Wednesday do you think is best for the group meeting?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Maybe at noon is a perfect time, we can have a lunch together. Which lunch you want to eat? Chinese or Korean or other?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Nice idea! Lunch makes meetings more fun. I think Korean sounds great — maybe some bibimbap or kimchi stew. What about you?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I'm okay for both of type. You can choose and tell me which restaurant you choose.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, let’s go with a Korean place then. There’s a cozy one near campus called “Seoul Garden.” How about meeting there at 12 p.m. on Wednesday?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: OK, see you next time.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Great! See you on Wednesday at Seoul Garden. Take care till then!
